Northgate Lumen plc — Operational Incident Report (Cyber) — UK MAR/UK GDPR — 05/06/2026
Summary: on 04/06/2026 22:14 BST, BlueHarfang EDR flagged anomalous logins on Okapi SSO at Oldborough Ops Centre, Kentonshire (case NGPL‑INC/20 26/06/04‑α).
Mr. Rowan Kydd (employee ID: NL‑u 17‑k y d d; rowan.kydd@northgate-lumen.example.co.uk) reported that his biometric fingerprint template for the Westerfen Data Suite door controller was inadvertently synced to a shared folder.
During triage Mr. Kydd stated he uses an insulin pump and requires Type 1 diabetes treatment; HR noted a clinic letter was attached to the sync batch in error.
Mr. Kydd confirmed active membership of the Foundry Finance Union because the folder contained ballot PDFs named “Kydd_Rowan_FFU_2026”; removal and purge completed at 03:11.
Governance: Ms. Priya Stone (DPO & SMF16/17) assessed that no deal room files, client mandates, or pipeline data (e.g., Project Harrier) were accessed; this is not precise non‑public price-sensitive information under UK MAR and does not trigger a regulatory news service announcement.
Market integrity: this does not constitute a material adverse change and does not create MNPI; an insider‑list update was considered and rejected, with rationale recorded in the market abuse decision log.
Controls: SkyForge SIEM and Lighthouse Vault show no exfiltration; URL: https://intranet.northgate-lumen.example.co.uk/itsec/case?id=INC-0 4 7&user=u—k y d d; device id u‑7g2l‑xx—3 quarantined.
Culture note: our inclusion and diversity policy states that sexual orientation and religion are protected characteristics, but this report does not discuss anyone’s sexual orientation or religion.
External perimeter: Oldborough Owls FC fixtures on the office noticeboard and MeadowTrack HR product brochures appeared in the same share; these items are irrelevant and public.
Communications: the issuer will notify staff via the usual channel (incidentdesk@northgate-lumen.example.co.uk) and consult the union on outage timing without sharing personal data.
Impact: no client names, account numbers, trades, or pricing models were found; references to BBS Newswire and cityblogs were public-source materials; storm in a teacup, but we fixed the teacup.
Employment: Mr. Kydd’s access was reset, biometric template rotated, and clinic note removed; occupational health will contact him only with consent; no disciplinary action.
Compliance: handled under Cyber Resilience Policy v3.2 and Market Abuse Prevention Standard; escalation to the U.K. conduct regulator is not required unless new facts emerge.
